message:
fix(responsiveness): poison-safe runtime lock — telemetry no longer goes silent after a panic-while-locked

Both step_once() and telemetry() used to short-circuit on a poisoned
runtime Mutex — step_once via `if let Ok(mut runtime) = self.runtime.lock()`
and telemetry() via `match self.runtime.lock() { Ok(...) => ..., Err(_) =>
empty }`. The practical effect is that any panic in any thread that
happened to be holding the runtime lock (e.g. a pathological snapshot
or a test that intentionally panics) would permanently mute the
governor: every subsequent tick's telemetry update would silently be
dropped, and every telemetry() read would return empty data, for the
rest of the process lifetime.

That's strictly worse than just reading through the poison. The
runtime state is tick-local: each sampler tick overwrites
last_snapshot / last_reason and append-or-rotates recent_decisions.
The state a poisoned lock holds is structurally valid — it was just
the *writer* that panicked before reaching a logical checkpoint. Even
if the content is one tick stale, continuing to update it from the
next tick onwards is the correct recovery.

Fix: replace both acquisition sites with
`.lock().unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner)` which
unconditionally yields the guard. Added explicit `drop(runtime)`
after each critical section so the guard's lifetime is obvious to
readers (and to help rust-analyzer annotate release points).

New regression test `telemetry_survives_mutex_poison` explicitly
poisons the mutex from a helper thread (spawn → acquire lock → panic →
join), asserts is_poisoned(), then drives 4 step_once() calls and
verifies:
  - ticks_total == 4 (atomics always update regardless of mutex state)
  - recent_decisions is non-empty (the Vec actually received writes
    through the poisoned lock)
  - last_reason is Some (the runtime struct was written to)
Before the fix this test would fail on the non-empty assertion since
the `if let Ok(...)` guard would skip every write.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
